summary: The reproduction starts FUXA v1.2.9 in its default posture and creates a new administrator account through `POST /api/users` without sending any credential.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated request to an admin-documented API route should succeed and the created administrator account should appear in the returned `/api/users` list.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned FUXA v1.2.9, started the server, posted a new admin user to `/api/users` without any token, and then received HTTP 200 from `/api/users` with the attacker-created `evohunt` account present in the response body.
verification_method: The container waits for `/api/version`, sends an unauthenticated `POST /api/users`, then fetches `/api/users` and checks for HTTP 200 plus the exact `"username":"evohunt"` marker. This distinguishes real anonymous admin access from a startup failure or a rejected request.
evidence_produced_by_run: The server responded to `POST /api/users` without any `x-access-token` header.
The returned user list contained the attacker-created `evohunt` administrator record.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
